user_message: The CSA escalated Wide World Importers to me -- can you build the narrative?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 08:27 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Wide World Importers

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 16:24 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 08:36 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is territory planning.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:27:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:32:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:36:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:38:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-C trap (Generates generic talking points); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-C failure mode (Generates generic talking points)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-C -- Generates generic talking points
